title: taraxacum pectinatum
intro: Taraxacum pectinatum, also known as the rough dandelion or rough bur marigold, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Taraxacum pectinatum include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, and its ability to boost the immune system. Taraxacum pectinatum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a dietary supplement in the form of capsules or tablets.
